included: Rollback expectations for any future Montgomery activation readiness work
Verification expectations for dependency and containment references

rollbackTriggers: Future activation readiness review identifies incomplete Montgomery dependency evidence
Future activation readiness review identifies containment uncertainty
Future release governance requests Montgomery package withdrawal before activation
Future verification detects unintended production registry, runtime, or boundary mutation attributable to Montgomery activation preparation

rollbackOwners.responsibility: Authorize rollback decision for Montgomery readiness work before any activation step.
Confirm Montgomery package-scoped artifacts and dependency references remain reversible.
Confirm rollback verification evidence and protected-system safeguards.
Coordinate stakeholder notice that rollback planning is documentation-only and no runtime action has executed.

rollbackSequence.name: Freeze Montgomery activation progression
rollbackSequence.expectation: Pause any future Montgomery activation readiness advancement until rollback review is complete.

rollbackSequence.name: Confirm package-scoped artifacts
rollbackSequence.expectation: Review Montgomery artifacts under assets/county-implementation/montgomery/ without moving files into data/ or production paths.

rollbackSequence.name: Verify dependency references
rollbackSequence.expectation: Confirm boundary, registry, package manifest, containment fixture, and containment validation references are documented; missing V577 registry/manifest files remain observations unless supplied by a separate approved package.

rollbackSequence.name: Validate protected-system state
rollbackSequence.expectation: Confirm protected systems remain unchanged and Montgomery remains not activated.

rollbackSequence.name: Document rollback evidence
rollbackSequence.expectation: Record assumptions, reversibility expectations, observations, and final rollback readiness decision.

rollbackSequence.name: Communicate rollback status
rollbackSequence.expectation: Notify release stakeholders of rollback readiness status and any dependency observations without implying runtime execution.

rollbackVerificationSteps: Confirm rollback procedure artifact exists and is valid JSON.
Confirm rollback validation artifact exists and records sequence completeness.
Confirm rollback evidence artifact exists and records assumptions and dependency references.
Confirm Montgomery boundary dependency reference is present and boundary data is not modified by V579.
Confirm containment fixture and validation references are present.
Confirm expected V577 registry and package manifest references are recorded, with workspace absence treated as an observation if files are not present.
Confirm no production registry records are created.
Confirm Montgomery activation state remains not_activated.
Confirm no application code, runtime behavior, Supabase, Liberty boundary data, historical systems, DriveTexas, or Transportation Intelligence changes occurred.
rollbackCommunicationExpectations: Describe V579 as rollback planning and evidence only.
Disclose dependency observations for missing V577 registry and package-manifest artifacts when applicable.
Do not communicate Montgomery as activated, onboarded, migrated, or production-ready.
Direct reviewers to the V579 validation and evidence artifacts before activation readiness work proceeds.
rollbackReviewRequirements: Review all generated V579 JSON artifacts for valid JSON.
Review documentation report for protected-system verification.
Review dependency observations before recommending activation readiness.
Require a separately approved milestone for any runtime, registry, migration, or activation action.
